file_insights.purpose: Package initialization and documentation for core model tests
key_components: List of models being tested: Organization, User, Branch, Role, Permission, Session, AuditLog

file_insights.purpose: Comprehensive unit tests for Organization and User core models

patterns_discovered.description: Uses factory classes (OrganizationFactory, UserFactory, etc.) for generating test data, enabling flexible and reusable test fixtures
patterns_discovered.benefit: Reduces boilerplate, improves test readability, centralizes fixture logic

patterns_discovered.description: Uses @pytest.mark.django_db decorator on test classes to enable database access in pytest
patterns_discovered.benefit: Clean integration with Django ORM, enables database transaction rollback between tests

patterns_discovered.description: Comprehensive testing of model properties (@property decorators) and methods alongside field validation
patterns_discovered.benefit: Ensures business logic is working correctly, not just data persistence

patterns_discovered.description: Tests for soft delete functionality including deleted_at tracking, is_deleted property, and manager filtering
patterns_discovered.benefit: Validates non-destructive deletion implementation with audit trail preservation

patterns_discovered.description: Tests cover normal paths, optional fields, edge cases (expired trials, suspension/activation cycles), and constraint violations (IntegrityError)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Comprehensive validation of model behavior under various conditions

gotchas_discovered.description: Model uses both default manager (excludes deleted) and all_objects manager (includes deleted). Tests must verify both querysets work correctly
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Tests explicitly check both Organization.objects and Organization.all_objects to ensure filtering logic

gotchas_discovered.description: created_at and updated_at are managed by Django. Tests must account for timing variations between before/after assertions
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Tests use timezone.now() with flexible comparisons (<=, >=) rather than exact equality

gotchas_discovered.description: Organization.settings is a JSONField that requires explicit refresh_from_db() after modifications to see persisted changes
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Tests consistently use refresh_from_db() after save() operations on settings

gotchas_discovered.description: is_on_trial property depends on timezone.now() comparison. Tests need to manage trial_ends_at relative to current time
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Tests use timedelta offsets (days=-1, days=+7) to ensure deterministic behavior

gotchas_discovered.description: Raw passwords are never stored; tests must use check_password() method to validate, not string comparison
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Tests use check_password() method rather than comparing hashed value directly

approach_outcome.summary: Created comprehensive unit test suite for Organization and User models with 37+ test methods covering field validation, properties, methods, soft delete functionality, and edge cases
test_coverage_areas: Model instantiation with required and optional fields
Custom methods (get_setting, set_setting, suspend, activate, verify_email, record_login)
Soft delete implementation (soft_delete, restore, is_deleted, manager filtering)
Inheritance behavior (AbstractBaseUser methods for User model)

recommendations.recommendation: Add performance tests for soft delete manager queries to verify proper indexing on deleted_at field
recommendations.rationale: Large organizations with many deleted records could have slow queries if deleted_at is not indexed

recommendations.recommendation: Add concurrent modification tests for settings JSONField to ensure thread-safe updates
recommendations.rationale: Concurrent updates to settings could cause lost updates if not properly handled

recommendations.recommendation: Add parametrized tests for status transitions (e.g., all valid status change combinations) to reduce code duplication
recommendations.rationale: Current tests cover suspend/activate but parametrized approach would scale better for additional statuses

recommendations.recommendation: Add test docstrings explaining expected behavior of business logic methods (e.g., why suspend clears suspension_reason on activate)
recommendations.rationale: Some test names don't clearly indicate expected state transitions

recommendations.recommendation: Consider adding factory fixtures for common test scenarios (e.g., 'suspended_org', 'unverified_user') to DRY up test setup
recommendations.rationale: Some tests repeat similar setup patterns that could be abstracted into specialized factories
